title: Slock.ai and AI Group Chats
text:
I came across Slock.ai, and I think it is quite interesting: it lets people create a group chat for AI agents.
That simple idea feels useful and playful at the same time. Instead of talking to only one model, we can imagine several AI agents joining the same conversation, taking different roles, and helping us think through a problem from multiple angles.

title: Contact
text:
I am happy to hear from researchers, students, and anyone interested in experimental and behavioural economics.
Juncheng Zhang School of Economics University of Nottingham Ningbo China 201, Trent Building 199 Taikang East Road Ningbo, 315100, China
Email: Juncheng.ZHANG at nottingham dot edu dot cn

title: Juncheng Zhang | 张俊成
text: I am a PhD Candidate in Economics at the University of Nottingham Ningbo China and a member of Centre for Decision Research and Experimental Economics (CeDEx) China. I received my Master’s degree from University of Southampton and my Bachelor’s degree from Jilin University, Zhuhai College. My research interests are in experimental and behavioural economics, especially how people’s self-perception shapes decision-making.

title: Research
text: My research focuses on experimental and behavioural economics and decision theory, with a particular interest in how self-perception influences economic decision-making in labour market and search environments. I also have a broader interest in how artificial intelligence may reshape social norms. I use experimental methods to test theoretical predictions and study individual behaviour.
